title: Aureline approval-ticket vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the approval_ticket_record, approval_ticket_audit_event_record, and provider_scope_resolution_result_record frozen in docs/adr/0010-connected-provider-browser-handoff-approval-ticket.md. Approval tickets are inspectable authority objects that bind user intent, policy, target identity, sandbox profile, and execution-context id to every provider mutation, credential projection, high-risk local mutation, and admin / policy change. Only the shell, the policy service, and the supervisor MAY mint tickets; AI flows, extensions, recipes, CLI scripts, browser companions, and remote helpers MAY request approval but MAY NOT grant themselves authority. Tickets carry redaction_class-declared fields; raw secret bytes, raw URLs, and raw delegated-token bodies never appear on the ticket body. The eventual approval-ticket crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new action_class, issuer_class, use_posture, high_risk_flag, denial_reason, audit_event_id, or redaction_class is additive-minor and requires an approval_ticket_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

approval_ticket_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this approval-ticket vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles.

issuer_class.description: Only the shell, the policy service, and the supervisor MAY mint approval tickets. Any other issuer is forged and denied.

provider_actor_class.description: The provider-actor class the ticket is spent as. 'unknown_actor_class' tickets are denied at spend time; a surface that cannot resolve the actor routes to a repair hook rather than issuing a ticket under a generic 'Connected' state.

action_class.description: Frozen action-class vocabulary. Every ticket names exactly one action class. Admits the named action and nothing broader; widening mints a new ticket with a fresh lineage entry.

use_posture.description: Frozen use-posture vocabulary. 'single_use' is the default; 'bounded_reuse' requires an explicit counter; 'session_scoped' is forbidden for release_publish and trust_or_policy_admin_change.

bounded_reuse_counter.description: Bounded-reuse counter. Present only when use_posture is bounded_reuse.

high_risk_flag.description: Frozen high-risk flag vocabulary. Every flag has an approval-time AND a spend-time gating rule; a flag without its rule is denied at issue with high_risk_gating_missing.

denial_dimension.description: Ticket denials and expiries MUST explain which dimension failed. Silent 'please try again' is forbidden.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason set for the approval-ticket plane. Denials fail closed; they MUST NOT silently retry and MUST NOT downgrade mutation mode.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the approval-ticket side of the provider_handoff stream.

human_summary.description: Human-legible paragraph the user confirmed at approval time.

machine_descriptor.description: Machine-readable command / mutation / projection descriptor. Shape is action-class-specific. MUST NOT contain raw secret bytes, raw URLs, or raw delegated-token bodies; those cross the RPC boundary as opaque refs only.

mutation_mode.description: Frozen mutation-mode vocabulary. Mode is disclosed at the point of intent. 'publish_now' and 'local_draft' MAY NOT collapse into a single control.

ticket_schema_version.description: Integer schema version pinned into the ticket so a replayed ticket is validated against the exact shape it was minted under.

actor_subject.description: Acting identity (user id, install id, delegated binding id, project-scoped grant id, or policy-injected service id).

issuing_surface.description: Where the ticket was issued from (shell prompt id, policy-decision id, supervisor path).

command_family_or_action_ref.description: Stable reference to the command family or provider action the ticket admits. A ticket admits the named action and nothing broader.

workspace_or_workset_scope_ref.description: ADR-0009 workset / slice / workspace id the ticket applies inside.

target_identity_ref.description: Execution target or provider target the ticket is bound to (ADR-0009 target-identity record id or connected_provider_record id).

sandbox_profile_or_capability_hash.description: Capability envelope the admitted action runs under.

projection_mode_ref.description: ADR-0007 projection mode when action_class is credential_projection; null otherwise. MUST reference a projection mode in the frozen ADR-0007 set.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Browser-handoff packet id when action_class is browser_handoff; null otherwise.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id the ticket runs inside.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch at issue (ADR-0008 / ADR-0009). A ticket presented at a later policy epoch is re-evaluated; silent continued-use is forbidden.

rememberable_scope.description: Non-null only when the user accepted a 'remember this decision' narrowing. Compiles to a narrow reusable rule plus renewable short-lived tickets, not to an unlimited bearer credential. Forbidden for release_publish, trust_or_policy_admin_change, and credential_projection of signing-class secrets.

high_risk_flags.description: Set of high-risk flags on this ticket. Every flag carries an approval-time AND a spend-time gating rule; a flag without its rule is denied at issue.

step_up_required_flag.description: When true, the ticket is not spendable until a fresh authenticator event clears it.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Optional ADR-0008 rollback-checkpoint ref the ticket commits to restoring on denial or failure. Required when high_risk_flags contains destructive_local_action.

preview_ref.description: Optional preview-record id the user approved against. Required for publish_now, release_publish, trust_or_policy_admin_change, high_risk_paste_or_injection, and local_workspace_mutation with destructive_local_action.

ticket_lineage.description: Ordered list of predecessor ticket ids when the ticket derives its authority from another ticket (for example, a deferred_publish_drain ticket inherits from the local_draft approval that queued the item).

revoke_epoch.description: Null until revoked; populated on revocation. Paired with revoke_reason.

revoke_reason.description: Populated on revocation with a typed reason.

audit_metadata.description: Authoritative on the audit stream. The ticket body MUST NOT be persisted in workspace files, profiles, sync exports, recipes, scaffolds, shell history, or support bundles; only opaque ticket_id refs cross those surfaces.

allOf.description: projection_mode_ref is required when action_class is credential_projection.
browser_handoff_packet_ref is required when action_class is browser_handoff.
bounded_reuse_counter MUST be present when use_posture is bounded_reuse, and MUST be absent otherwise.
session_scoped use_posture is forbidden for release_publish and trust_or_policy_admin_change.
rememberable_scope is forbidden for release_publish and trust_or_policy_admin_change.
revoke_reason MUST be present when revoke_epoch is non-null.
rollback_checkpoint_ref is required when high_risk_flags contains destructive_local_action.
preview_ref is required for the mutation-class action classes named in ADR-0010.

approval_ticket_audit_event_record.description: An observable event on the approval-ticket side of the provider_handoff audit stream. Raw secret bytes, raw URLs, and raw delegated-token bodies are forbidden.

requested_action.description: Stable reference to the provider action that was evaluated.

target_object_ref.description: Opaque reference to the provider-side object the action targets.

effective_scopes.description: Effective scope set combining provider-declared scopes, Aureline policy bundles, object-level target rules, and local trust posture.

policy_locks.description: Policy-bundle locks that narrowed or denied the action.

fallback_or_browser_only_reason.description: Populated when the action is routed to browser handoff or to local_draft because no direct mutation path is reachable; empty string otherwise.

admitted_mutation_mode.description: The mutation mode this resolution admits. Empty only on a pure inspect_only surface.

admitted_approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque reference to the approval ticket that admits the resolution when grant_resolution_reason is one of the 'allowed' values.

provider_scope_resolution_result_record.description: One provider_scope_resolution_result per requested-action / target pair, reused by desktop, CLI, companion, support export, and mutation-journal. Silent widening between actor classes is forbidden.
